hook.text: On June 8, 2021, Reddit, the New York Times, and Twitch all went offline at the same time.

buildup.text: Fastly, a major CDN, had a global outage. One customer config change triggered a hidden software bug.

discovery.text: The bug crashed 85% of Fastly's network. Websites served by Fastly showed 503 errors worldwide.

twist.text: Fastly fixed it in 49 minutes. But those minutes revealed how much of the web depends on a single CDN.

climax.text: The incident reignited debate about internet centralization. Too few companies carry too much traffic.

punchline.text: One CDN bug downed half the internet's top sites.

quiz.question: What caused the Fastly outage in 2021?
options: A customer config change triggered a latent software bug
A massive power failure at Fastly's data centers
